places.name: Sagano Bamboo Forest
places.description: The sound of swaying stalks in this stunning grove has been named a governmentally recognized sound.

places.name: Nishiki Market
places.description: This 700-year-old street food market features baby octopus and soy donuts.

places.name: Yokai Street
places.description: This neighborhood thoroughfare is haunted by dozens of homemade monsters.

places.name: Otagi Nenbutsu-ji Temple
places.description: Twelve hundred stone carvings guard this off-the-beaten-path Buddhist temple.

places.name: Sanjūsangen-dō
places.description: 1,001 carved statues of the Buddhist God of Mercy, each with a unique face.

places.name: Ryōan-ji
places.description: The quintessential Japanese rock garden has existed for more than 500 years.

places.name: Nishiki Market
places.description: This 700-year-old street food market features baby octopus and soy donuts.

places.name: Starbucks Ninenzaka Yasaka Chaya
places.description: This Kyoto Starbucks takes coffee drinkers back in time.

places.name: Ichimonjiya Wasuke
places.description: Established over a thousand years ago, this family-run teahouse continues to serve skewers of sweet rice cake to this day.
